rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks in valid component type or implementation scopes
Use assign only for implementation-level bindings when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are available
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, for example `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic in AGREE contracts
generic_rules: Maintain scope consistency by placing annex blocks in the appropriate component or implementation context
Use valid AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for declarations and expressions
Avoid direct use of AADL property names in AGREE expressions unless explicitly exposed as ports or constants
Use AGREE logical operators (and, or, not, =>) for clear contract expressions
Ensure all referenced identifiers in AGREE expressions are visible in the current scope
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annex blocks are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};`
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions without explicit typed ports or constants
Do not use invalid AGREE scalar type names (e.g., integer, boolean) in declarations
Do not place assumptions or guarantees in scopes where the referenced identifiers are not visible
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
